topic: Investigate why focused deep-research bug-finding can surface more bugs than the deep-review workflow, and identify changes that would make deep-review less surface-level.

focus: Inventory deep-review/deep-research surfaces and first shallowness hypotheses
summary: Deep-review appears shallower because its loop rotates across broad dimensions, has smaller/looser defaults, validates high-severity claims after discovery, and lacks explicit bug-class saturation or producer/consumer enumeration gates; deep-research is more question-driven and enumeration-friendly.

focus: Reducer, state format, convergence references, and tests: documented rigor versus machine-checked guarantees
summary: Deep-review has enforceable shape, severity, blocked-stop, and coarse graph convergence guarantees, but it does not generally machine-check producer/consumer inventory, invariant hypotheses, bug-class saturation, or negative-test search before dimensions count as covered.

focus: Real completed deep-review artifact survey for rich field presence and bug-class coverage
summary: Real review artifacts show rich fields are present but uneven: findingDetails and graphEvents are optional in practice, findingsNew shape drifts, counterevidence is sparse, producer/consumer evidence is mostly prose, and explicit bug-class coverage is effectively absent from structured finding details.

focus: Deep-review prompt-pack and agent candidate-generation pressure
summary: Deep-review prompt and agent surfaces force evidence, severity, scope, claim adjudication, and output consistency, but they do not force bug-class hypotheses, invariant sweeps, producer/consumer tracing, negative-test searches, or adversarial matrix construction before findings are validated. The strongest matrix language exists in fix-completeness after findings, while deep-research more naturally externalizes hypotheses through focus, invariant, observation, edge, and ruled_out records.

focus: Deep-research mechanics to transfer into deep-review
summary: Research mechanics should transfer as candidate-generation pressure: narrow candidate focus, structured hypothesis/invariant/producer/consumer/negative-test rows, reducer-owned ruled-out directions, evidence enumeration for clean paths, and candidate-class convergence gates before severity adjudication.

focus: Deep-review scope selection and file/resource targeting
summary: Deep-review discovers broad file scopes and rotates through dimensions, but it does not require a ranked file/flow/hotspot queue, mandatory graph or semantic discovery, or a target-selection rationale before dispatch. Resource maps improve traceability coverage, and graph convergence can block stops when graph data exists, but neither currently drives initial bug-hunting target selection.

focus: Evaluate deep-review output contracts and synthesis behavior
summary: Deep-review synthesis can hide shallow searches because verdict, severity counts, active finding registry, planning packet, and dashboard risk sections are strongly structured, while ruled-out/null-search evidence is optional, late, and unstructured. A less surface-level review needs a required search ledger or candidate coverage matrix that preserves rejected hypotheses and missed-class risks alongside active findings.

focus: Turn accumulated evidence into ranked design options and acceptance criteria
summary: Ranked candidate improvements by leverage, risk, affected surface, and verification cost. Highest leverage is a mandatory candidate/search ledger enforced by stricter post-dispatch validation, followed by reducer/synthesis persistence of null-search evidence, target-selection narrowing, graph vocabulary expansion, convergence candidate-saturation gates, report changes, and only then higher iteration defaults.

focus: Define verification strategy for highest-leverage deep-review improvements
summary: Verification should prove depth at four layers: post-dispatch schema enforcement, prompt-pack contract visibility, reducer/dashboard persistence, graph candidate-coverage stop blockers, and manual sandbox scenarios with seeded bugs. Existing tests cover artifact presence, basic JSONL shape, graph dimension blockers, and blocked-stop dashboarding; new tests must require candidate/search-ledger rows, target selection, null-search evidence, and candidate-aware convergence.

focus: Final convergence pass: synthesize accumulated evidence, resolve weak claims, close remaining questions, and produce roadmap inputs
summary: Final convergence supports a stable diagnosis: focused deep-research finds more bugs because it preserves hypothesis search and ruled-out evidence, while deep-review preserves dimensions, severity, active findings, and verdicts more strongly than candidate generation. Highest-leverage remediation is a versioned searchLedger with targetSelection/searchCoverage, prompt-visible contract, post-dispatch validation, reducer/dashboard/report persistence, then graph/convergence expansion and seeded-bug tests. Remaining uncertainty is threshold calibration, not the core ordering.

focus: Stress-test the versioned searchLedger and targetSelection recommendation: required fields, naming, minimal row shape, trivial-review exemptions, and backwards compatibility.
summary: The searchLedger recommendation is sound but needs tighter versioned enforcement: canonical searchLedger naming, explicit reviewDepthApplicability, targetSelection separate from scope discovery, minimal cited ledger rows, graphless fallback proof, legacy warn-only reads, and seeded tests that reject checkbox rows.

focus: Stress-test post-dispatch validation: what must fail, warn, or pass for findingsNew, findingDetails, searchLedger rows, evidenceRefs, dispositions, and versioned enforcement.
summary: Post-dispatch validation needs a versioned hard-error/warn/pass profile: v2 complete standard/complex review rows should fail wrong severity-count shapes, shallow active findingDetails, absent or uncited searchLedger rows, invalid dispositions, broken finding links, missing target-selection proof, and state/delta drift; legacy, trivial, graphless-with-fallback, and non-complete rows need explicit warning/pass handling.

focus: Stress-test reducer, registry, dashboard, and report persistence: how null-search evidence, search debt, ruled-out candidates, and clean-path proof should survive synthesis.
summary: Reducer/dashboard/report persistence is the fragile link: current reducer state is finding-centric, dashboard verdicts are severity-centric, ruled-out prose collapses clean proof with dead ends, report synthesis lacks a Search Ledger destination, and validation does not prove reducer survivability.

graphEvents.label: Reducer drops ledger-shaped evidence unless promoted into registry state
Dashboard PASS can hide search debt
Ruled-out persistence collapses clean proof and dead ends

focus: Stress-test graph vocabulary and convergence gates: candidate saturation, BUG_CLASS/INVARIANT/PRODUCER/CONSUMER/TEST nodes, graphless fallback, and STOP_BLOCKED behavior.
summary: Graph/convergence expansion is useful but must be ledger-led: current review graph schema and workflow discard BUG_CLASS/INVARIANT/PRODUCER/CONSUMER/TEST kinds, current graph signals cannot measure candidate saturation, graphless STOP behavior contradicts the docs, and blocked_stop needs named candidate/search gates before graph blockers can prove depth.

graphEvents.label: Review graph vocabulary expansion is inert unless schema and workflow allow new candidate node kinds
Current review graph signals do not measure candidate saturation
Graphless STOP behavior contradicts graceful degradation docs

focus: Final continuation convergence: refine implementation order, seeded behavior tests, rollout thresholds, residual risks, and exact follow-up acceptance criteria across all recommendations.
summary: Final convergence tightens the follow-up into staged acceptance gates: seeded tests must precede each slice, searchCoverage must define ledger obligations, rollout needs validator warnings before hard enforcement, graphless fallback must be explicit, search debt must affect dashboard/report posture, and graph vocabulary should remain ledger-led with exact persistence criteria.

graphEvents.label: Seeded tests must gate every implementation slice, not arrive after graph work
searchCoverage obligations are required to prevent checkbox searchLedger rows
Rollout requires a warning surface before v2 hard enforcement expands
Graphless fallback must be an explicit candidate/search proof mode
Search debt must affect dashboard and report posture
No standard or complex clean review may PASS without cited candidate-search coverage
